issues: The draft headline 'Sockeyes Even Series With Broncos' is not confirmed by the OCR — no headline text is visible in the OCR, only the byline and article body.

The draft describes this as the 'Centennial Cup Western Canada junior hockey final series' but the OCR explicitly names it the 'Abbott Cup' (the Western final), not the Centennial Cup. The Centennial Cup is the national championship; the Abbott Cup is the Western Canada final.
The draft score of 5-3 is correct per OCR.

The description says 'Centennial Cup Western Canada junior hockey final series' in both short and long — this is factually wrong; the OCR calls it the Abbott Cup series (Western Canada final). The Centennial Cup is a separate, later national tournament.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat Humboldt Broncos 5-3 to even their Abbott Cup (Western Canada junior hockey final) series at one game each.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes, B.C. Junior League champions, defeated the hometown Humboldt Broncos 5-3 on April 22, 1987, evening their best-of-seven Abbott Cup Western Canada junior hockey final series at one game each. According to the Sun News Services report, "an attitude adjustment" was all Richmond needed to get back on the winning track. Game three was scheduled for that same evening in Humboldt before the series was set to shift venues.
review.notes: No headline is visible in the OCR — the draft headline should be cleared or treated as editorially inferred. The critical factual error is calling this the Centennial Cup series; the OCR clearly names it the Abbott Cup (Western Canada final). The Centennial Cup national championship came later in May 1987. The byline "Sun News Services" is confirmed by the OCR.
